settings.value: OpenFeature Provider documentation for Superposition across all supported languages.
Type-safe, cascading configuration files with SuperTOML - an extension of TOML that adds JSON Schema validation and CSS-inspired override cascading.
Real-world applications and examples built using Superposition for context-aware configuration and experimentation.
Represents conditional criteria used for context matching. Keys define dimension names and values specify the criteria that must be met.
Represents conditional criteria used for context matching. Keys define dimension names and values specify the criteria that must be met.
Configuration overrides that replace default values when context conditions are met. Keys represent configuration keys and values are the override data.
Generic key-value object structure used for flexible data representation throughout the API.
Generic key-value object structure used for flexible data representation throughout the API.
Represents conditional criteria used for context matching. Keys define dimension names and values specify the criteria that must be met.

Represents conditional criteria used for context matching. Keys define dimension names and values specify the criteria that must be met.
Configuration overrides that replace default values when context conditions are met. Keys represent configuration keys and values are the override data.
Indicates that the operation succeeded but the webhook call failed. The response body contains the successful result, but the client should be aware that webhook notification did not complete.

The successful operation result that would have been returned with HTTP 200, serialized as an untyped/raw JSON document. The structure logically corresponds to the operation's normal output type, but is modeled as Document since this single error is shared across multiple operations with different output shapes.
Generic key-value object structure used for flexible data representation throughout the API.

value.name: count
value.description: Number of items to be returned in each page.

schema.description: Number of items to be returned in each page.

value.name: page
value.description: Page number to retrieve, starting from 1.

schema.description: Page number to retrieve, starting from 1.

value.name: all
value.description: If true, returns all requested items, ignoring pagination parameters page and count.

schema.description: If true, returns all requested items, ignoring pagination parameters page and count.

value.name: name
value.description: Filter by experiment group name (exact match or substring, depending on backend implementation).

schema.description: Filter by experiment group name (exact match or substring, depending on backend implementation).

value.name: created_by
value.description: Filter by the user who created the experiment group.

schema.description: Filter by the user who created the experiment group.

value.name: last_modified_by
value.description: Filter by the user who last modified the experiment group.

schema.description: Filter by the user who last modified the experiment group.

value.name: sort_on
value.description: Field to sort the results by.

value.name: sort_by
value.description: Sort order (ascending or descending).

schema.description: Sort order enumeration for list operations.
schema.title: SortBy

value.name: group_type
value.description: Filter by the type of group (USER_CREATED or SYSTEM_GENERATED).

schema.description: Filter by the type of group (USER_CREATED or SYSTEM_GENERATED).

value.name: dimension_match_strategy
value.description: Strategy to follow while filter items based on the context

schema.description: Strategy to follow while filter items based on the context
schema.title: DimensionMatchStrategy

value.name: if-modified-since
value.description: While using this, 304 response is treated as error, which needs to be handled separately by checking the response code of the http response. This is required to make sure that clients can cache the response and avoid unnecessary calls when there are no updates.

schema.description: While using this, 304 response is treated as error, which needs to be handled separately by checking the response code of the http response. This is required to make sure that clients can cache the response and avoid unnecessary calls when there are no updates.

Indicates that the operation succeeded but the webhook call failed. The response body contains the successful result, but the client should be aware that webhook notification did not complete.

The successful operation result that would have been returned with HTTP 200, serialized as an untyped/raw JSON document.
